mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue projections and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery regimes, with a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 205 signals entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending capacity and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor strength signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above trend reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue generation, which directly supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% during late-cycle expansions signals manufacturing contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO falls > 0.5% during late-cycle phases, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample validation.
